concepts.title: The Letter She Never Sent
concepts.description: A woman in her late thirties sits at a worn kitchen table, morning light streaming through gauze curtains, her hand resting on an envelope she's been holding for years. The stamp is faded. The address is in handwriting she no longer uses. A cold cup of coffee has left a ring on the wood beside her. She is looking at something we cannot see — perhaps a window, perhaps nothing.
concepts.unanswered_question: Who was the letter for, and why did she never send it?

concepts.title: The Door Left Open
concepts.description: A young man stands in a doorway, his back to us, one hand still on the doorframe. Beyond him is blinding afternoon light. The room behind him is dim, intimate — a bed unmade, a glass of water on the nightstand. He has stopped mid-departure. Something has made him pause. The light from outside renders him almost silhouette.
concepts.unanswered_question: What made him stop? Will he leave or turn back?

concepts.title: Mother's Hands, Flour Dusted
concepts.description: Close crop on weathered hands kneading bread dough on a floured wooden board. Wedding ring thin with decades. Kitchen light from a single window. In the soft background, a child's drawing is taped to a cabinet — faded, curling at the edges. The hands have paused. They are still. Something has been remembered.
concepts.unanswered_question: Whose hands are these? Where is the child who made that drawing?

concepts.title: The Last Light in the Hall
concepts.description: A long hallway in an old house, photographed from the far end. Only one door is open, spilling warm light onto the wooden floor. A woman's shadow falls into the hall but she herself is not visible — only the edge of her dress, a hand on the doorframe. The rest of the hall is dim, doors closed. Dust motes suspended.
concepts.unanswered_question: What room is she in? Why is she the only light still on?

concepts.title: The Mirror Knows
concepts.description: A bathroom mirror, slightly fogged. A woman has wiped a clear space with her palm and we see her face through it — looking at herself but not seeing, eyes focused somewhere internal. The edges of the mirror remain fogged, creating a natural vignette. A single toothbrush in a cup. Tiles cracked in one corner.
concepts.unanswered_question: What has she just learned? What is she preparing herself to do?

concepts.title: His Father's Chair
concepts.description: An empty leather armchair by a window, late afternoon light. The leather is worn in specific places — where hands rested, where a head leaned back. A folded newspaper on the side table, glasses on top. Standing in the doorway, half in shadow, a middle-aged man looks at the chair. He has not sat in it. Perhaps he never will.
concepts.unanswered_question: How long has the chair been empty? Why can't he bring himself to sit?

concepts.title: The Photograph in the Drawer
concepts.description: A woman's hands holding an old photograph, slightly creased. We see the photo from behind — only the white back and her fingers. She sits on the edge of a bed. An open drawer beside her, fabric visible. The light is soft, indeterminate — could be dawn or dusk. Her face is not shown, but her posture speaks.
concepts.unanswered_question: Who is in the photograph? Why was it hidden in the drawer?

concepts.title: The Window She Watches From
concepts.description: A woman stands at a rain-streaked window, but the rain on the glass seems to fall upward. Her reflection is slightly out of sync with her movement — turned toward us while she faces away. The room behind her is ordinary: a couch, a lamp, a book left open. But the light is wrong. Too golden. Too still.
concepts.unanswered_question: Is this a memory or a dream? Whose perspective are we seeing?

concepts.title: The Meal Set for Two
concepts.description: A small dining table set for two, but only one chair is occupied. The other chair is pulled out, napkin unfolded, wine poured — as if someone just stepped away. But the light suggests they've been gone for years. Candles burned down to nubs. Dust on the untouched plate. The seated figure — a woman in her sixties — looks at the empty seat with tenderness, not grief.
concepts.unanswered_question: Who is the meal for? How long has she been waiting?

concepts.title: The Suitcase by the Door
concepts.description: A packed suitcase sits by a front door, but no one is leaving. A woman sits on the bottom stair, coat on, looking at the suitcase as if it were a living thing. The hallway is narrow, wallpaper faded. A mirror reflects only the back of her head. The light comes from above — a single bare bulb. She has been sitting there for a long time.
concepts.unanswered_question: Why hasn't she left? What is she waiting for?

concepts.title: The Garden Through Glass
concepts.description: A woman presses her palm flat against a window, looking out at a garden in full summer bloom. But her reflection shows winter clothes, breath fogging the glass. The garden outside is impossibly vivid — the inside muted, gray. Her eyes are closed. She is not seeing the garden; she is remembering it.
concepts.unanswered_question: What season is it really? Is the garden memory or longing?

concepts.title: The Book She Stopped Reading
concepts.description: An open book on a bedside table, pages turning slightly in a breeze from an unseen source. A woman asleep in the bed behind it, face peaceful but streaked with dried tears. The room is ordinary — lamp, water glass, reading glasses — but the light is that impossible pre-dawn blue. A bookmark lies on the floor, never placed.
concepts.unanswered_question: What did she read that made her cry? Why did she stop?
